input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete plan for setting up .NET MAUI on Windows 11 targeting Android + Windows.

---

## Step 1 — Install the .NET SDK

Download the latest .NET SDK (MAUI requires .NET 8+):

```powershell
# Check if dotnet is already installed
dotnet --list-sdks

# Install via winget (preferred)
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.8
```

## Step 2 — Install MAUI Workloads

```powershell
# Discover available workloads
dotnet workload search maui

# Install the MAUI workload (includes Android, Windows, iOS stubs)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installation
dotnet workload list
```

## Step 3 — Install Android SDK Components

The MAUI workload pulls in the Android SDK, but you may need to accept licenses and verify components:

```powershell
# Find where the Android SDK was installed
$androidSdkPath = (Get-ChildItem "${env:ProgramFiles(x86)}\Android\android-sdk" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) `
    ?? (Get-ChildItem "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)

# If not found, check the dotnet MAUI android SDK path
# The workload typically installs it here:
$dotnetAndroidSdk = Join-Path $env:USERPROFILE ".android"
Get-ChildItem $dotnetAndroidSdk -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue

# Discover installed Android platform versions dynamically
$sdkmanager = Get-ChildItem -Path $androidSdkPath, "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk" `
    -Filter "sdkmanager.bat" -Recurse -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select -First 1
& $sdkmanager.FullName --list

# Accept all licenses
& $sdkmanager.FullName --licenses

# Install required components (adjust API level to latest listed)
& $sdkmanager.FullName "platform-tools" "platforms;android-35" "build-tools;35.0.0" "emulator"
```

## Step 4 — Set Up an Android Emulator

```powershell
# List available system images
& $sdkmanager.FullName --list | Select-String "system-images"

# Install a system image (pick the latest stable x86_64)
& $sdkmanager.FullName "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"

# Create an AVD
$avdmanager = Get-ChildItem -Path $androidSdkPath, "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk" `
    -Filter "avdmanager.bat" -Recurse -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select -First 1
& $avdmanager.FullName create avd -n "Pixel_MAUI" -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" --device "pixel_6"

# Verify
& $avdmanager.FullName list avd
```

## Step 5 — Enable Windows App SDK (for Windows target)

```powershell
# The MAUI workload includes WinUI/Windows App SDK support
# But ensure the Windows App SDK runtime is present:
winget search "Windows App SDK"
winget install Microsoft.WindowsAppSDK.1.6

# Enable Developer Mode (required for sideloading)
Start-Process "ms-settings:developers"
# Then toggle "Developer Mode" ON in the UI
```

## Step 6 — (Optional) Install Visual Studio Build Tools

If you want CLI-only builds without full Visual Studio:

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools

# Then add required components via the installer workloads:
# - ".NET Multi-platform App UI development"
# - "Universal Windows Platform development"
```

If using full Visual Studio instead:

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community
# Then select the "MAUI" workload in the VS Installer UI
```

## Step 7 — Create and Verify a Project

```powershell
# Create a new MAUI app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Android emulator (start emulator first)
$emulator = Get-ChildItem -Path $androidSdkPath, "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk" `
    -Filter "emulator.exe" -Recurse | Select -First 1
Start-Process $emulator.FullName -ArgumentList "-avd Pixel_MAUI"
# Wait for boot, then:
dotnet build -f net8.0-android -t:Install -t:Run
```

## Step 8 — Validate Everything

```powershell
# Check all workloads are healthy
dotnet workload repair

# Run the MAUI diagnostic check
dotnet new maui-blazor -n DiagCheck --dry-run

# Verify Android SDK env var is set
[System.Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", "User")
# If not set:
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME",
    "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk", "User")
```

---

**Key notes:**
- Replace `android-35` / `net8.0` with whatever `--list` / `workload search` reports as latest
- Hyper-V or WHPX must be enabled for fast Android emulation (`Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V-All`)
- If using .NET 9+, substitute `net9.0-*` target frameworks accordingly
data.reasoningText: The user wants a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11, targeting Android and Windows. They can't run commands now, so they want all the PowerShell commands laid out. Let me provide a comprehensive guide.
